assertions.reason: Two consecutive node scripts/apply-patches.mjs runs exited 0, kept dist/type and dist/esm hashes identical, and the second run emitted no @enbox/agent patch line.

With the d.ts drifted away from both HdIdentityVault and IdentityVault markers, apply-patches left it byte-identical and returned without corrupting the file; the diagnostic included the installed @enbox/agent version.
After temporarily moving dist/types/enbox-user-agent.d.ts aside, apply-patches exited 0 and did not recreate or corrupt the missing target.
dist/types/enbox-user-agent.d.cts is absent in the installed @enbox/agent package, and apply-patches explicitly guards the optional path with existsSync.
The optional src/enbox-user-agent.ts patch is present and matches the widened IdentityVault declarations in dist/types.
The @enbox/agent widening lives only in scripts/apply-patches.mjs as an embedded read/replace/write patcher, patches/ is empty, and git tracks no node_modules content.
After bun add -D typescript@5.8.3, the widened IdentityVault declarations remained present in both dist/types and src without any manual patch-script rerun.

summary: Validated 7 assigned patch-robustness assertions on jest-local: 7 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Baseline frozen install, repeated patch runs, drift/missing-file simulations, optional layout checks, sanctioned-artifact checks, and incremental bun add restoration all completed; working tree status matched the initial state apart from expected validator outputs.
